page: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by the Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 192 virtual CPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The dedicated CPU allocation operates at a clock speed of 3.2 GHz, supported by a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 105 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, and it operates on the Nitro hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 10% of tested systems. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers top-tier read bandwidth, reaching 13,862.96 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 455.06 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The processor's advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), contribute to top-tier performance in cryptographic operations, physics simulations, and database operations. Additionally, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference, particularly in prompt processing for large models and text generation for medium-sized models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m7i.48xlarge offers high resource density and high-speed DDR5 memory without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a highly efficient option for workloads that rely on network-attached storage and require massive parallel processing power. The instance is well-suited for large-scale database operations, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 96 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor running at 3.2 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory, delivering a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on the Nitro hypervisor and a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results highlight top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory read bandwidth, and outstanding database operation throughput. Advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, enables efficient cryptographic execution and CPU-based LLM inference. This instance is highly optimized for large-scale database hosting, high-throughput web serving, and parallelized enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 vCPUs, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor, optimized for high-throughput database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7i.48xlarge features 192 vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose platform featuring 192 Intel Xeon vCPUs and DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.

768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory read bandwidth performance
Advanced instruction support including AVX-512 and Intel AMX

Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
